Off Calendar Jan-08-2018 Continued To Mar-14-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Special Motion Of Defendants Direcpark, Llc And Shuli Yao To Strike Plaintiffs Complaint Pursuant To Ccp 425.16
Off Calendar Jan-23-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DIRECPARK, LLC, SHULI YAO's Special Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Complaint Pursuant To Ccp 425.16.

Defendants DirecPark LLC and Shuli Yao's anti-SLAPP motion to strike the sole cause of action for conversion in the complaint filed by plaintiff John Hanlin is denied. Defendants failed to satisfy their first prong burden of showing that Mr. Hanlin's claim arises from activity protected by the anti-SLAPP statute. Mr. Hanlin alleges that defendants converted his BMW by wrongfully taking possession of, denying him access to, assuming ownership of, and selling the BMW. Nowhere in the complaint is there any reference to any petitioning or other protected speech activity. Nor does the complaint refer to a small claims judgment, requests made to the DMV or a lien sale. Defendants' first prong argument would require that this court re-write the complaint to include references to the lien sale and other matters not mentioned therein. Case law precludes this court from doing so. (Medical Marijuana, Inc. v. ProjectCBD.com (2016) 6 Cal. App. 5th 602, 621 ("It is not our role to engage in what would amount to redrafting of the first amended complaint in order to read that document as alleging conduct that supports a claim that has not in fact been specifically alleged, and then assess whether the pleading that we have essentially drafted could survive the anti-SLAPP motion directed at it.")).

Mr. Hanlin is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, mtorreano@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/HK)
